title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
While passing through a side door near a storeroom filled with recreational items, Miss Bondell and Bill encounter Constable Butter, who is inspecting golf clubs. Inspector Bardell joins the group shortly after.

Later, Jimmy Sessage, feeling downcast and wanting to avoid discussions regarding the Santa Fe border dispute, approaches Loran Wade in the garden. Jimmy proposes marriage, but Loran declines, stating it is not the right time because they are not yet safe. Jimmy agrees, noting that while they have secured a document from the Aviation Minister, they have yet to catch the elusive "seven o'clock." Loran expresses fear regarding this figure and the death of Gerry, but Jimmy, claiming to be a genius, vows to catch him and his accomplices. Jimmy also mentions that he has gained the favor of Mrs. Kut through his charm and a recent injury.

Loran reveals that Bundel suggested she stay at the Chimney House. Jimmy encourages this so she can monitor Bundel’s unpredictable schemes. Loran suggests that Bill might be performing his own detective work, noting his fury when he saw Lomax holding Bundel's hand, though Jimmy remains skeptical of Bill's intelligence. Jimmy instructs Loran to return to the Chimney House with Bondel to prevent her from visiting the Seven Club.

Jimmy then approaches Mrs. Kut in the garden. While discussing his arm injury and the risk of septicemia, Jimmy feigns innocence to gather information. Mrs. Kut reveals that Sir Osward has rented the Duke of Alton's house in Latherbury, a large and gloomy residence. She then reminisces about her previous cheerful home in Yorkshire.
